topic: Plugin-internal API surface that S3 exposes for S4 to consume
direction: S3 owns the SDK transport; S4 imports a small named API to drive it

onWillAppear.signature: (handler: (event: {context: string, action: string, payload: {settings: object, coordinates?: object, controller: 'Encoder'|'Keypad'}}) => void) => void
onWillAppear.description: Registers a callback invoked once per willAppear SDK event after registerPlugin has succeeded.

onWillDisappear.description: Registers a callback invoked once per willDisappear SDK event.

onDidReceiveSettings.description: Registers a callback invoked when the SDK delivers updated settings (e.g., user changed a PI field).

sendFeedback.signature: (context: string, payload: {title: string, artist: string}) => void
sendFeedback.description: Sends a setFeedback frame to the SDK WebSocket with the given context and payload. Payload shape is constrained by C-S2-S4. If the WebSocket is not open, the call is a no-op (logged at debug).

log.description: Sends a logMessage frame to the SDK WebSocket. Used by S4 for diagnostic output.

lifecycle.startup: Calling code (main.js entry) imports sdk.js, registers handlers, then calls sdk.start() which parses argv, opens the WebSocket, sends registerPlugin, and begins dispatching events.
lifecycle.no_action_effects_for_input_events: touchTap, dialRotate, dialDown, dialUp events are received by sdk.js and either silently dropped or routed through internal logging only. S4 never registers a handler for them. This enforces IP3 (display-only) at the API boundary.

verification: Static check: S4's module imports only the names listed under 'exports' from sdk.js. Behavior check: when sdk receives a touchTap, no S4-owned code path runs that would call sendFeedback or any other action-effect send.
